items.title: Apple loses challenges against EU rules to curb Big Tech

items.title: SK Hynix, Samsung Drag KOSPI Into Bear Market as Memory-Chip Selloff Deepens
items.summary: The world’s hottest index has been dragged into a technical bear market as the memory-chip selloff gathered pace Wednesday.  The KOSPI index closed down 5.4% in South Korean trading as its two largest constituents  SK Hynix  and  Samsung Electronics  dropped 5.7% and 6.3%, respectively.  The KOSPI may have entered technical bear market but it remains one of the best-performing major global indexes this year, up 72%, while the S&P 500 is up 9.6% in 2026 so far.
